title: Figure 3 from: Pan X, Chen Z, Chen Y, Lei J, Chen X, Wu T, Li Y (2025) Two new species of Xylaria (Xylariaceae, Ascomycota) associated with fallen leaves in Hainan Tropical Rainforest National Park, China. MycoKeys 112: 17-34. https://doi.org/10.3897/mycokeys.112.140487
author: Pan, Xiaoyan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 3 Xylaria tuberculosa (HAFFR 123) a stromata on leaves b, d stromatal surface and ostioles (arrow) c section through stroma, showing perithecia e, f ascus in 1% SDS g ascospores in water h ascal apical ring in Melzer's reagent i ascospore showing a slightly shorter than spore-length straight germ slit in water j ascospore showing non-cellular appendages in 1% SDS (arrows) k, l ascospores under SEM. Scale bars: 0.5 cm (a); 200 µm (b–d); 10 µm (e–j, l); 5 µm (K).
